### Comment by [@kshalot](https://github.com/kshalot) â€” 2026-02-13T16:38:09Z

@ichekrygin @olekzabl Thank you for your thoughtful comments so far. The last two days I went back to the drawing board to come up with a more refined design that will be more targeted towards this KEP's goal.

TL;DR of the changes:
1. I propose expanding the Workload API instead of adding annotations.
1. For now, focus on the MultiKueue case rather than generalizing. Generalize in beta if suitable.
1. Rely on the `PreemptionGate` struct instead of `Condition` to track signaling (TBD).

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
```

  I'm afraid reusing this annotation may lead to some complications.
  
  Consider a MK setup with 1 manager and 2 workers such that:
  - `worker1` has 2 Flavors (`flavor1` & `flavor2`) and [FlavorFungibility](https://kueue.sigs.k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster_queue/#flavorfungibility) set to
    ```
    whenCanBorrow: MayStopSearch
    whenCanPreempt: MayStopSearch
    ```
  - `worker2` has 1 Flavor (`flavor3`) based on a provisioning request.
  
  Now, consider a workload which can preempt in `flavor1` or borrow in `flavor2` or provision in `flavor3`.
  How should it be treated by MultiKueue?
  
  IIUC:
  
  - If it's submitted with KEP-8729's "never preempt" flag, then preempting is no-go. 
    Thus, on `worker1`, ignore the option in `flavor1` and proceed to `flavor2`, to borrow.
    This will likely be quicker than provisioning in `flavor3`, so the workload will end up on `worker1`, `flavor2`.
  - If it's submitted without that flag, then on `worker1` it should (I guess?) _plan for preempting_, that is, report to the manager that it's gated - and not consider `flavor2`. 
    In the meantime, the `worker2` copy will (at some point) provision successfully, so (as long as it succeeds before your timeout) the workload will end up on `worker2`, `flavor3`.
  
  But, with the proposed approach, Kueue on the worker won't know which case it's in. Unless it checks the workload on the manager (though I'd assume that's not your plan).
  
  This is just a mental experiment, maybe I'm wrong. (But then, maybe there are other tricky scenarios). 
  
  Maybe we can agree that it's all not so relevant, and simplicity of "unified API" is the decisive gain.
  But I'd like us to reflect on that.

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
+The manager cluster's MultiKueue controller that watches the replicated Workload objects will observe the condition and make a decision whether to remove the annotation from
+the replica, allowing it to proceed.
+
+The controllers responsible for dispatching workloads in a MultiKueue setup or creating virtual workloads for concurrent admission will be responsible for adding the
+appropriate preemption gates to the workloads they manage.
+
+If a preemption fails for some reason or the workload is not admitted after preemption, a **timeout mechanism** will ensure that the gate is eventually removed for other replica workloads so that
+another worker gets a chance to preempt. If a worker was ungated, the `PreemptionGateTimeout` elapsed and the workload is still pending, another worker can be considered for ungating.
+This prevents a single failing preemption from blocking all others.
+
+```mermaid
+sequenceDiagram
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Manager->>+Worker_2: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_1, Worker_1: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_1->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    Manager->>+Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    Note over Manager, Manager: No Workload Preempted Yet: ungate
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Remove Preemption Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Preempt
+    Worker_2->>Worker_2: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_2, Worker_2: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_2->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    critical
+        Manager->>Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    option Timeout Elapsed
+        Manager->>+Worker_2: Remove Preemption Gate
+        Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Preempt
+    option Timeout Did Not Elapse: N Seconds Left
+        Manager->>Manager: Requeue Ungating Logic In N Seconds
+    end
+```
+
+### User Stories
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a MultiKueue administrator, I want to maximize the resource usage of my system.
+One team in my company regularly submits high priority jobs to the clusters, which causes system-wide
+preemptions across many workers, halting the progress of jobs of other teams.
+
+I want that team's jobs to still be promptly admitted, but without causing distruptions.
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+1. The main risk of this proposal is the potential for deadlocks or starvation if the ungating logic is flawed.
+For example, if the preemption synchronization controller fails to ungate a workload or the preemption fails, it could be blocked indefinitely.
+This can be mitigated by implementing a timeout mechanism to re-queue the ungating decision.
+
+2. The limitation of using the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation is that it will make it impossible for the users to express MultiKueue
```

  That is a very good point an a clear indication that two KEPs cannot use the very same "contextless" annotation. Maybe the KEP should go directly to the field based API with named preemption gates?

+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
```

  It is also important to highlight a related, but distinct, inefficiency: sub-optimal placement in the presence of heterogeneous worker capacity. Even when using an Incremental, as opposed to AllAtOnce, dispatch strategy, a workload may be admitted on a worker that requires preemption while another worker could have admitted the same workload without any preemption at all. As a result, limiting preemption fan-out alone does not fully address the problem, as the chosen placement can still be unnecessarily disruptive. This further motivates handling preemption coordination automatically within MultiKueue, rather than relying solely on dispatch strategy semantics.

- **keps/8303-multikueue-synchronized-preemption/README.md:48** by [@ichekrygin](https://github.com/ichekrygin) â€” 2026-02-04T22:49:18Z

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
```

  I would frame it differently - avoid unnecessary workloads disruptions in MK context.

+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
```

  Unless I am misinterpreting the comment, fundamentally a WorkloadAdmissionConstraint is a binary admission predicate: the outcome is whether the workload is admitted or not. It is not intended to differentiate between flavors or influence flavor selection logic. From that perspective, whether the constraint is expressed via an annotation or via a future spec or gate API is largely an implementation detail.
  
  Applied to the example above, if a workload is submitted with a `cannot-preempt` constraint, the semantics are straightforward: as long as there exists at least one flavor, on any worker, that can satisfy the workload without preemption, the workload should be considered admissible. The presence of other flavors that could admit the workload via preemption, or interactions with flavor fungibility and stop-search heuristics, should not invalidate admission once a single admissible placement exists.

+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+

+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
```

  The name `PreemptionGated` was initially confusing to me. On first read, it sounds like a condition on a workload that is *about to be preempted* and is being held off, rather than a workload that *could trigger preemption* but is temporarily prevented from doing so. I had to re-read the paragraph to understand that this is meant to signal â€śready to preempt others, but gated.â€ť It might be worth considering a name that more clearly reflects that this is about gating the workloadâ€™s ability to **initiate** preemption, or adding a short clarifying sentence to make that distinction explicit. 
  
  Have you considered `AdmissionGated`?

- **keps/8303-multikueue-synchronized-preemption/README.md:70** by [@ichekrygin](https://github.com/ichekrygin) â€” 2026-02-04T23:07:42Z

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
+The manager cluster's MultiKueue controller that watches the replicated Workload objects will observe the condition and make a decision whether to remove the annotation from
+the replica, allowing it to proceed.
+
+The controllers responsible for dispatching workloads in a MultiKueue setup or creating virtual workloads for concurrent admission will be responsible for adding the
+appropriate preemption gates to the workloads they manage.
+
+If a preemption fails for some reason or the workload is not admitted after preemption, a **timeout mechanism** will ensure that the gate is eventually removed for other replica workloads so that
+another worker gets a chance to preempt. If a worker was ungated, the `PreemptionGateTimeout` elapsed and the workload is still pending, another worker can be considered for ungating.
+This prevents a single failing preemption from blocking all others.
+
+```mermaid
```

  This relates to my earlier comment in the *Motivation* section. The diagram does not capture the case where one of the workers can admit the workload without requiring any preemption at all, for example, `worker2`. In the illustrated flow, MultiKueue would still proceed with preemption on `worker1`, even though a non-preemptive placement may already be available. This seems to miss an important class of scenarios and could be misleading to readers.

### Review by [@ichekrygin](https://github.com/ichekrygin) (commented) â€” 2026-02-04T23:23:28Z

- **keps/8303-multikueue-synchronized-preemption/README.md:112** by [@ichekrygin](https://github.com/ichekrygin) â€” 2026-02-04T23:23:28Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
+The manager cluster's MultiKueue controller that watches the replicated Workload objects will observe the condition and make a decision whether to remove the annotation from
+the replica, allowing it to proceed.
+
+The controllers responsible for dispatching workloads in a MultiKueue setup or creating virtual workloads for concurrent admission will be responsible for adding the
+appropriate preemption gates to the workloads they manage.
+
+If a preemption fails for some reason or the workload is not admitted after preemption, a **timeout mechanism** will ensure that the gate is eventually removed for other replica workloads so that
+another worker gets a chance to preempt. If a worker was ungated, the `PreemptionGateTimeout` elapsed and the workload is still pending, another worker can be considered for ungating.
+This prevents a single failing preemption from blocking all others.
+
+```mermaid
+sequenceDiagram
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Manager->>+Worker_2: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_1, Worker_1: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_1->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    Manager->>+Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    Note over Manager, Manager: No Workload Preempted Yet: ungate
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Remove Preemption Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Preempt
+    Worker_2->>Worker_2: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_2, Worker_2: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_2->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    critical
+        Manager->>Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    option Timeout Elapsed
+        Manager->>+Worker_2: Remove Preemption Gate
+        Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Preempt
+    option Timeout Did Not Elapse: N Seconds Left
+        Manager->>Manager: Requeue Ungating Logic In N Seconds
+    end
+```
+
+### User Stories
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a MultiKueue administrator, I want to maximize the resource usage of my system.
+One team in my company regularly submits high priority jobs to the clusters, which causes system-wide
+preemptions across many workers, halting the progress of jobs of other teams.
+
+I want that team's jobs to still be promptly admitted, but without causing distruptions.
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+1. The main risk of this proposal is the potential for deadlocks or starvation if the ungating logic is flawed.
+For example, if the preemption synchronization controller fails to ungate a workload or the preemption fails, it could be blocked indefinitely.
+This can be mitigated by implementing a timeout mechanism to re-queue the ungating decision.
+
+2. The limitation of using the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation is that it will make it impossible for the users to express MultiKueue
```

  Unless I am misunderstanding the risk being raised, I tend to disagree that this is a clear indication that the two KEPs cannot share the same annotation.
  
  In the WorkloadAdmissionConstraint model, user intent is expressed explicitly at the Job level in a MultiKueue context and propagates transparently to the corresponding Workload on the manager and to the worker replicas. In this case, a `cannot-preempt` constraint simply means that only non-preempting placements are admissible. If one or more such placements exist, the workload is admitted using the existing MultiKueue mechanisms. If none exist, the workload remains pending, it does not enter a system-wide â€śeventually preemptâ€ť state.
  
  I agree that there is a potential starvation risk here, but that risk is inherent to the constraint itself and represents an intentional trade-off: users can explicitly opt out of preemptive admission in order to avoid disruption.
  
  That said, I donâ€™t think this annotation necessarily needs to be user-facing in the context of this KEP. An alternative is to treat it as a dispatcher or controller implementation detail and structure admission in explicit phases. Conceptually, this could look like:
  
  * **Phase 1**: attempt to admit the workload under a non-preempting constraint.
    * If one or more admissions succeed, proceed with the existing MultiKueue flow.
    * If no admissions succeed, continue to Phase 2.
  * **Phase 2**: retry admission with preemption gated and synchronized, as described in this proposal.
  
  This keeps the semantics of admission constraints intact, avoids overloading user intent with coordination mechanics, and does not preclude moving to a field-based API once we have more operational experience.

+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
```

  @ichekrygin
  
  - I agree that annotation/FG/API is an implementation detail. Indeed, I care about the desired semantics.
  - I also agree that `cannot-preempt` should work in the way you described. 
  
  What concerns me is whether `cannot-preempt` should be reused in this KEP. 
  
  That's because, in the situation described in this KEP (i.e. the customer did _not_ express a "never preempt" constraint; they allow preemption in principle but want to avoid multiple preemptions), the desired semantics may be different.
  
  For me, the intuitive desired worker semantics in this KEP would be "treat preempting as an allowed action - just normally - and just withhold preempting until you get a green light from the manager".
  OTOH, as discussed in [another comment thread](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kueue/pull/8985#discussion_r2766265680) here, reusing `cannot-preempt` for this KEP leads rather to "allow preempting but treat it as a very last resort" (as outlined by you in the Phase 1 & Phase 2 summary). 
  And I think these two semantics diverge (as my above example tries to demonstrate).
  
  So, I see a likely tradeoff between:
  
  - "unified design" that re-uses the `cannot-preempt` annotation for this KEP
  - respecting the details of some existing mechanics like FlavorFungibility.
  
  I don't know which choice is better. I'd like us to choose consciously.

+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->

+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)

+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
```

  I reworded it with something that kind of combines both of your suggestions? PTAL if it is clearer now.

### Review by [@kshalot](https://github.com/kshalot) (commented) â€” 2026-02-05T13:24:03Z

- **keps/8303-multikueue-synchronized-preemption/README.md:112** by [@kshalot](https://github.com/kshalot) â€” 2026-02-05T13:24:02Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
+The manager cluster's MultiKueue controller that watches the replicated Workload objects will observe the condition and make a decision whether to remove the annotation from
+the replica, allowing it to proceed.
+
+The controllers responsible for dispatching workloads in a MultiKueue setup or creating virtual workloads for concurrent admission will be responsible for adding the
+appropriate preemption gates to the workloads they manage.
+
+If a preemption fails for some reason or the workload is not admitted after preemption, a **timeout mechanism** will ensure that the gate is eventually removed for other replica workloads so that
+another worker gets a chance to preempt. If a worker was ungated, the `PreemptionGateTimeout` elapsed and the workload is still pending, another worker can be considered for ungating.
+This prevents a single failing preemption from blocking all others.
+
+```mermaid
+sequenceDiagram
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Manager->>+Worker_2: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_1, Worker_1: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_1->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    Manager->>+Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    Note over Manager, Manager: No Workload Preempted Yet: ungate
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Remove Preemption Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Preempt
+    Worker_2->>Worker_2: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_2, Worker_2: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_2->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    critical
+        Manager->>Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    option Timeout Elapsed
+        Manager->>+Worker_2: Remove Preemption Gate
+        Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Preempt
+    option Timeout Did Not Elapse: N Seconds Left
+        Manager->>Manager: Requeue Ungating Logic In N Seconds
+    end
+```
+
+### User Stories
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a MultiKueue administrator, I want to maximize the resource usage of my system.
+One team in my company regularly submits high priority jobs to the clusters, which causes system-wide
+preemptions across many workers, halting the progress of jobs of other teams.
+
+I want that team's jobs to still be promptly admitted, but without causing distruptions.
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+1. The main risk of this proposal is the potential for deadlocks or starvation if the ungating logic is flawed.
+For example, if the preemption synchronization controller fails to ungate a workload or the preemption fails, it could be blocked indefinitely.
+This can be mitigated by implementing a timeout mechanism to re-queue the ungating decision.
+
+2. The limitation of using the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation is that it will make it impossible for the users to express MultiKueue
```

  @mwielgus I figured that the easier way to deliver a single-cluster preemption guarantee for MK will be to piggy-back on the proposed annotation, since the gating mechanism will look largely the same. Introducing an API at this stage does not incur any "implementational overhead" since the logic would remain largely the same. I simply got second thoughts after some of the previous conversations whether we are certain that the proposed API will cover all cases.
  
  --- 
  
  IMO there is a lot of merit to what @olekzabl and @mwielgus are saying - the semantics are becoming blurry when considering flavor fungibility and have to be clearly defined in this KEP.
  
  Working upon @olekzabl's example from https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kueue/pull/8985#discussion_r2765473401, let's consider:
  1. `workload1` being submitted, it matches `flavor1` and `flavor2`.
  2. `flavor1` is full, the workload won't fit without preemptions.
  3. `flavor2` has room for the workload.
  
  A workload arrives with `cannot-preempt` and `whenCanPreempt: MayStopSearch`.
  
  From the POV of KEP-8729 (Admission Time Constraints), it should skip `flavor1` and proceed to `flavor2`, because it can **never** trigger a preemption. Quoting @ichekrygin:
  > ... if a workload is submitted with a cannot-preempt constraint, the semantics are straightforward: **as long as there exists at least one flavor, on any worker, that can satisfy the workload without preemption, the workload should be considered admissible.**
  
  From the POV of this KEP, it's less clear. It could either:
  1. Decide to preempt, encounter the gate, wait for permission.
      * Consistent with the requested flavor fungibility policy.
  2. Continue to `flavor2`.
      * Consistent with the semantics of Admission Time Constraints.
      
  Option 2 doesn't require any distinct implementation, but it pretty much forces MultiKueue workers to always work in the `whenCanPreempt: TryNextFlavor` mode, ignoring the specified flavor fungibility policy.
  
  Option 1 seems like the more better choice for MultiKueue.
  
  ---
  
  I still see a significant overlap between those features, that's why I was hesitant to lock into a concrete API proposal.

+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals

+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.

+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
+The manager cluster's MultiKueue controller that watches the replicated Workload objects will observe the condition and make a decision whether to remove the annotation from
+the replica, allowing it to proceed.
+
+The controllers responsible for dispatching workloads in a MultiKueue setup or creating virtual workloads for concurrent admission will be responsible for adding the
+appropriate preemption gates to the workloads they manage.
+
+If a preemption fails for some reason or the workload is not admitted after preemption, a **timeout mechanism** will ensure that the gate is eventually removed for other replica workloads so that
+another worker gets a chance to preempt. If a worker was ungated, the `PreemptionGateTimeout` elapsed and the workload is still pending, another worker can be considered for ungating.
+This prevents a single failing preemption from blocking all others.
+
+```mermaid
+sequenceDiagram
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Manager->>+Worker_2: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Admission Loop

+### User Stories
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a MultiKueue administrator, I want to maximize the resource usage of my system.
+One team in my company regularly submits high priority jobs to the clusters, which causes system-wide
+preemptions across many workers, halting the progress of jobs of other teams.
+
+I want that team's jobs to still be promptly admitted, but without causing distruptions.
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+1. The main risk of this proposal is the potential for deadlocks or starvation if the ungating logic is flawed.
+For example, if the preemption synchronization controller fails to ungate a workload or the preemption fails, it could be blocked indefinitely.
+This can be mitigated by implementing a timeout mechanism to re-queue the ungating decision.
+
+2. The limitation of using the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation is that it will make it impossible for the users to express MultiKueue
```

  After some consideration, I think it would be worth it to share some logic under the hood, but don't re-use the annotation. Even if the logic will be exactly the same under the hood, having a separate would at least illustrate the semantical differences.
  
  What I'm on the fence on is whether this should be a dedicated API or simply a different annotation... At this point it's hard for me to see whether there is any functional difference between them, especially with all three related features being at the stage of the KEP (this, Admission Time Constraints, Concurrent Admission).

  This is a good point worth consideration, but I'm wondering whether it should be included in this KEP or not. It does fit under "synchronized preemptions", but it is not addressed in the KEP at all, so I'm hesitant to include it in the motivation.

+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
```

  `AdmissionGated` has the disadvantage that it might be hard to tell what exactly happened programatically. For example, if there's a similar mechanism for borrowing, it would be the same `AdmissionGated` reason. The message would be different, but using constant condition messages for logic seems like a bad idea.
  
  I figured that since it's a reason for the `QuotaReserved` condition then it wouldn't be ambiguous, since it implies admission time in a way. But we could make it more explicit if more people deem it unclear.

+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
```

  I think my concern is mostly about framing rather than expanding the scope of this KEP. In the MultiKueue context, â€śpreemption synchronizationâ€ť feels like a narrow slice of a broader problem, which is sub-optimal workload placement under heterogeneous worker capacity.
  
  That sub-optimality shows up in a couple of closely related ways: workloads get preempted on workers where they arguably shouldnâ€™t, and workloads get placed in a way that requires preemption even though non-preemptive placement options exist elsewhere. Both of these arise from the same admission and placement decision point, and both are addressed, at least partially, via preemption gating in very similar contexts.
  
  Iâ€™m not suggesting that this KEP should solve the general placement problem. Rather, my intent was to highlight this as motivating context: limiting or synchronizing preemption fan-out alone does not fully address unnecessary disruption if the initial placement decision is already sub-optimal. Calling this out helps explain why longer-term solutions likely need to live in MultiKueue itself, beyond dispatch strategy semantics, even if this KEP only tackles a more focused piece of the problem.

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+

+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)

+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
```

  I think that â€śPreemptionGatedâ€ť is growing on me. The more context I have around this KEP, the more the term makes sense, especially when interpreted as a reason attached to `QuotaReserved`.
  
  My initial hesitation, and to some extent what Iâ€™m still probing, is about first-read perception. If a user or operator looks at a workload and sees `PreemptionGated`, the immediate mental model might be â€śthis workload is about to be preempted but is being held back,â€ť rather than â€śthis workload could trigger preemption, but is currently prevented from doing so during admission.â€ť
  
  That said, given that this is a *reason* on the `QuotaReserved` condition, it already implicitly scopes the meaning to admission-time behavior, which does reduce the ambiguity quite a bit. With that framing, I think the name is defensible and probably fine.
  
  If others still find this confusing, an alternative could be to add a short clarifying sentence in the KEP describing the semantics explicitly, rather than trying to encode all of that nuance into the condition name itself.

### Review by [@ichekrygin](https://github.com/ichekrygin) (commented) â€” 2026-02-05T16:34:01Z

- **keps/8303-multikueue-synchronized-preemption/README.md:112** by [@ichekrygin](https://github.com/ichekrygin) â€” 2026-02-05T16:34:01Z
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
+<!-- toc -->
+- [Summary](#summary)
+- [Motivation](#motivation)
+  - [Goals](#goals)
+  - [Non-Goals](#non-goals)
+- [Proposal](#proposal)
+  - [User Stories](#user-stories)
+    - [Story 1](#story-1)
+  - [Risks and Mitigations](#risks-and-mitigations)
+- [Design Details](#design-details)
+  - [API Definition](#api-definition)
+    - [(alpha) Workload Annotations](#alpha-workload-annotations)
+    - [(alpha) Extending the <code>QuotaReserved</code> Condition](#alpha-extending-the-quotareserved-condition)
+    - [(alpha) <code>PreemptionGateTimeout</code> Configuration](#alpha-preemptiongatetimeout-configuration)
+    - [(beta) Workload API](#beta-workload-api)
+  - [MultiKueue Controller](#multikueue-controller)
+  - [Test Plan](#test-plan)
+    - [Unit Tests](#unit-tests)
+    - [Integration tests](#integration-tests)
+  - [Graduation Criteria](#graduation-criteria)
+- [Implementation History](#implementation-history)
+- [Drawbacks](#drawbacks)
+- [Alternatives](#alternatives)
+  - [Define a proper Workload API for alpha](#define-a-proper-workload-api-for-alpha)
+<!-- /toc -->
+
+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
+- Propose a generalized mechanism that can be extended to other scenarios.
+
+### Non-Goals
+
+- Optimize logic for choosing which worker cluster should be allowed to preempt, beyond a simple first-come-first-served approach (i.e. considering any "preemption cost").
+- Propose an analogous mechanism of borrowing synchronization.
+
+## Proposal
+
+The proposed solution is to use the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation proposed in KEP-8729 as the "preemption gate" and introduce a
+`PreemptionGated` reason to the `QuotaReserved` condition in the Workload's status, which will be used to signal that it's ready to preempt but was gated.
+The manager cluster's MultiKueue controller that watches the replicated Workload objects will observe the condition and make a decision whether to remove the annotation from
+the replica, allowing it to proceed.
+
+The controllers responsible for dispatching workloads in a MultiKueue setup or creating virtual workloads for concurrent admission will be responsible for adding the
+appropriate preemption gates to the workloads they manage.
+
+If a preemption fails for some reason or the workload is not admitted after preemption, a **timeout mechanism** will ensure that the gate is eventually removed for other replica workloads so that
+another worker gets a chance to preempt. If a worker was ungated, the `PreemptionGateTimeout` elapsed and the workload is still pending, another worker can be considered for ungating.
+This prevents a single failing preemption from blocking all others.
+
+```mermaid
+sequenceDiagram
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Manager->>+Worker_2: Replicate Workload With Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_1, Worker_1: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_1->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    Manager->>+Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    Note over Manager, Manager: No Workload Preempted Yet: ungate
+    Manager->>+Worker_1: Remove Preemption Gate
+    Worker_1->>+Worker_1: Preempt
+    Worker_2->>Worker_2: Admission Loop
+    Note over Worker_2, Worker_2: Insufficient Quota: preemption gated
+    Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Update QuotaReserved Condition
+    Worker_2->>+Manager: Change Event: PreemptionGated
+    critical
+        Manager->>Manager: Check Workload Group Preemption Timeout
+    option Timeout Elapsed
+        Manager->>+Worker_2: Remove Preemption Gate
+        Worker_2->>+Worker_2: Preempt
+    option Timeout Did Not Elapse: N Seconds Left
+        Manager->>Manager: Requeue Ungating Logic In N Seconds
+    end
+```
+
+### User Stories
+
+#### Story 1
+
+As a MultiKueue administrator, I want to maximize the resource usage of my system.
+One team in my company regularly submits high priority jobs to the clusters, which causes system-wide
+preemptions across many workers, halting the progress of jobs of other teams.
+
+I want that team's jobs to still be promptly admitted, but without causing distruptions.
+
+### Risks and Mitigations
+
+1. The main risk of this proposal is the potential for deadlocks or starvation if the ungating logic is flawed.
+For example, if the preemption synchronization controller fails to ungate a workload or the preemption fails, it could be blocked indefinitely.
+This can be mitigated by implementing a timeout mechanism to re-queue the ungating decision.
+
+2. The limitation of using the alpha `kueue.x-k8s.io/cannot-preempt` annotation is that it will make it impossible for the users to express MultiKueue
```

  Before diving in, to avoid ambiguity: in this discussion Iâ€™m treating â€śannotationâ€ť and â€śAPI fieldâ€ť as semantically equivalent representations, i.e. different encodings of the same intent. For simplicity, Iâ€™ll refer to this uniformly as **API** below.
  
  I think weâ€™re broadly aligned on one key point: if the *mechanism* under the hood is the same, then the question of whether we diverge at the API level is secondary. That said, I believe itâ€™s worth trying to converge as early and as far upstream as possible, unless we can clearly articulate why divergence is necessary.
  
  Concretely, today I see two possible flows:
  
  **This KEP (MultiKueue synchronized preemption):**
  
  * MK: Job â†’ MK internals â†’ Workload (API) â†’ Worker Workload (API) â†’ AdmissionConstraint logic
  
  **Admission Time Constraints KEP:**
  
  * MK: Job (API) â†’ Workload (API) â†’ Worker Workload (API) â†’ AdmissionConstraint logic
  * Single-cluster: Job (API) â†’ Workload (API) â†’ AdmissionConstraint logic
  
  The first thing worth calling out is that, in the context of *this* KEP, there is no Job-level API at all. The feature is not user-facing, it is entirely driven by MultiKueue internals. That already avoids one major source of semantic overloading around user intent.
  
  The second point is about semantics at the **Workload** level. If we choose to use a different Workload API here, I think we are implicitly assuming that the downstream behavior or guarantees will meaningfully diverge from Admission Time Constraints in the future. If thatâ€™s the case, it would be really helpful to call out those expected differences explicitly in this KEP, so weâ€™re clear on what weâ€™re buying with the additional surface area.
  
  If, on the other hand, the intent is that both features ultimately express the same constraint, namely â€śthis workload must not cause preemption during admission,â€ť then converging on a shared Workload-level API seems like the more natural direction, even if the triggering mechanism differs (user intent vs MK coordination).
  
  I agree that flavor fungibility and search semantics make the interaction subtle, and that this KEP highlights some of that tension. But to me, that argues more for tightening and clarifying the shared semantics than for immediately splitting APIs. We can still keep the MultiKueue-specific coordination logic fully internal, without exposing it as user-facing intent, while aligning on a common Workload-level signal.
  
  In short, Iâ€™m not opposed to divergence if we can point to concrete semantic differences that require it. Absent that, early convergence at the Workload level feels like the safer default, especially with multiple related KEPs evolving in parallel.

+## Summary
+
+Currently, when a high-priority workload is dispatched to multiple worker clusters in a MultiKueue setup, it can trigger preemptions on all of them simultaneously.
+However, since in the end the workload will only run on a single cluster, the preemptions on the other clusters are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources and disruptions.
+
+This KEP proposes a mechanism to synchronize preemptions in these cases and considers how this feature can be extended to other scenarios.
+It introduces a way for workloads to signal that they want to trigger a preemption and discusses the concept of "preemption gates" in the Workload API.
+
+## Motivation
+
+In a MultiKueue environment, a high-priority workload dispatched to multiple worker clusters can trigger simultaneous preemptions.
+For instance, a workload sent to three clusters using the `AllAtOnce` strategy might initiate preemptions on all three.
+Since the workload can only be admitted to one cluster, the preemptions on the other two are unnecessary and lead to wasted resources by
+halting running workloads and then having to re-admit them. This problems grows with the amount of deployed worker clusters and should be taken into account by MultiKueue automatically.
+
+Moreover, a general preemption gating/synchronization mechanism can be used to handle other scenarios in the Kueue ecosystem.
+
+### Goals
+
+- Increase resource usage in a MultiKueue environment by eliminating system-wide preemptions that disrupt running workloads.
```

  It does, thank you for updating. That said đź™‚
  
  In light of [this comment](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kueue/pull/8985#discussion_r2769506880), we should probably state this more explicitly in the Goals or Non-Goals (or both): this KEP focuses strictly on a subset of preemption scenarios where preemption is required in **all** worker clusters.
  
  Iâ€™d also like to explicitly call out that this KEP does **not** address, intentionally or otherwise, cases where preemption is required in **some** worker clusters, while other worker clusters could admit the same workload without any preemption at all.

### Review by [@mwielgus](https://github.com/mwielgus) (commented) â€” 2026-02-05T20:11:38Z

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# KEP-8303: MultiKueue Synchronized Preemption
+
